commit.message:
Fix compound IPA merge falsely joining user-written separate words

splitCompoundsInText("butterfly") splits to "butter fly" for eSpeak,
then the compound merge in runTextParser rejoins the IPA. But it
also merged "butter fly" (user-written as two words), making it
sound identical to "butterfly" with no word boundary gap.

Fix: use \x1F (Unit Separator) instead of space between compound
halves. The merge step now only fires at \x1F boundaries, leaving
regular-space-separated words alone. eSpeak treats \x1F identically
to space (verified with espeak-ng CLI).

This also fixes "lighthouse keeper" vs "light housekeeper" — they
previously sounded the same because the merge blindly joined any
adjacent words matching a compound map key.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Move string methods to StringPrototype, remove compiler string dispatch

- Add 20+ string methods to StringPrototype: toLowerCase, toUpperCase,
  trim, trimStart, trimEnd, split, replace, replaceAll, charAt, indexOf,
  lastIndexOf, substring, startsWith, endsWith, includes, repeat, match,
  search, padStart, padEnd, codePointAt, charCodeAt
- Remove builtin_strings.go (transformStringMethod) entirely
- Remove transformBuiltinMethod and transformCollectionMethod
- Remove all string/collection method dispatch from expressions.go
- Add []*JSValue case to From() for proper slice-to-array conversion
- All method calls on JSValue now go through .MethodCall() → prototype chain
- Clean up jsvalueSliceLocals on scope pop to prevent flat-map pollution
- Scope-aware slice local wrapping: check current scope for rest params

Remaining transpile issues: string literals calling methods (" ".repeat())
and subscript access on match results (str.match(re)[0]) need separate
fixes in the compiler.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(winget): add Windows Package Manager publishing support

Add comprehensive winget publishing infrastructure:

1. Winget manifest files (.winget/):
   - A3SLab.Box.yaml - Version manifest
   - A3SLab.Box.installer.yaml - Installer configuration
   - A3SLab.Box.locale.en-US.yaml - Localization and metadata

2. GitHub Actions workflow (publish-winget.yml):
   - Automatic trigger on release
   - Manual workflow dispatch support
   - Downloads release asset and computes SHA256
   - Updates manifest files dynamically
   - Validates manifests with wingetcreate
   - Submits PR to microsoft/winget-pkgs

3. PowerShell submission script (scripts/submit-to-winget.ps1):
   - Manual submission tool
   - Automatic SHA256 computation
   - Manifest validation
   - PR creation to winget-pkgs

4. Documentation (docs/WINGET_PUBLISHING.md):
   - Three publishing methods (auto, script, manual)
   - Detailed step-by-step instructions
   - Troubleshooting guide

5. Integration with release workflow:
   - Automatically triggers winget publishing after release

Users can now install a3s-box via:
  winget install A3SLab.Box

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(napi): replace panics with error returns to prevent WASM traps (#1055)

## Summary

- Convert `panic!()` and `unwrap()` calls in the NAPI binding layer to
proper `napi::Result` error returns, preventing unrecoverable WASM
`unreachable` traps in WebContainer/WASI environments
- Change `From` impls to `TryFrom` for `Restriction` and
`TsconfigOptions`, propagating errors through `ResolverFactory::new` and
`clone_with_options`
- Replace `unwrap()` with `unwrap_or_default()` in tracing
initialization
- Bump minimum napi version from `3` to `3.8` to include upstream bug
fixes

related #635

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Ajout du kit TIZ_TH11 pour sous-traitance

Nouveau kit à assembler TIZ_TH11 avec 7 composants:
- 1x 00062_LP_0.7/1.7 (Presostat LP)
- 1x 00062_HP_26 (Presostat HP)
- 1x 6.04726.0000 (Filtre Emerson)
- 1x 01413 (Sběrač chladiva 1,6l)
- 1x 6.04677.0000 (Průhledítko Sanhua)
- 1x TGEN2,5_134 (Ventil expanzní)

- Move .md documentation files to docs/ folder
- Reorganize project structure for better maintainability

- Set DEBUG = True in settings.py to disable HTTPS redirect in development
- Fix stacking context issues preventing fixed positioning

  each ClaudeBrain.evaluate_entry call
- config.py: ai_local_model_enabled/url/name fields with env override + validation
- brain.py: local model branch in initialize() with health-check → cloud fallback
- bot.py: Ouroboros check wired into position loop; geometry analysis wired into
  _evaluate_and_execute; dump_detector cleanup on exit
- positions.py: trailing_stop_override_pct field; check_exit_conditions uses
  per-position override when set

ci: make ML deps optional, drop sharding & simplify pipeline (Issue #ARCH-7) (#999)

* ci: make ML deps optional and simplify CI pipeline (Issue #ARCH-7)

Install overhead (~2min) dominated every CI job while actual tests took
~30s. Root cause: torch (364MB), scipy, transformers pulled in by
sentence-transformers but mocked in all tests.

- Move scikit-learn, sentence-transformers, datasketch, numpy to
  optional [tool.poetry.group.ml.dependencies]
- Drop pytest-split sharding (18 test jobs → 2)
- Drop Python 3.12 from matrix (keep 3.11 min + 3.13 latest)
- Lint job: pip install ruff directly, no poetry/venv needed
- All CI jobs install with --without ml

gracefully skip when ML deps are not in the environment.

Both files imported cosine_similarity from clustering at module level,
which fails when sklearn is not installed. Move to local import inside
the functions that use it, matching the existing pattern.

commit.message:
Restore body and low-end fullness for Spotify-grade masters (v9.7.3)

Three changes to bring back the deep fullness of pro label masters:

1. LowMid cuts reduced ~60% (-2.5/-1.5 dB → -1.0/-0.5 dB) — previous
   cuts at 350Hz were scooping out warmth/body. Dynamic EQ still handles
   actual mud when AI detects it.

2. LowMid Q widened from 1.4 (surgical) to 0.8 (console-style) — gentle
   body shaping instead of narrow frequency scooping.

3. Sub shelf moved from 40Hz to 60Hz — 40Hz barely reproduces on earbuds
   and car stereos. 60Hz is where Spotify/Apple bass actually lives.
